card.title: OnlyFans Tax in Australia: Protect Your Privacy & Maximise Your Returns
card.excerpt: ABN, GST, Deductions & Business Structure — A Complete Guide for Creators

en.intro: OnlyFans creator income can grow fast — and your tax obligations grow with it. The ATO has significantly increased its focus on income from digital platforms and subscription-based content, meaning compliance is no longer optional. The good news: with the right structure, you can protect your privacy, minimise your tax, and stay fully compliant. Here is what you need to know.

sections.title: Is OnlyFans Income a Business or a Hobby?
paragraphs: Under ATO guidelines, OnlyFans income is almost always classified as Business Income — not a hobby. This applies even if you are still building your subscriber base, earning inconsistently, or treat it as a side income. Once you are providing content in exchange for payment, it is a business transaction.
What this means practically:
bullets: You must declare all income on your tax return
You should register an ABN as a Sole Trader
You are responsible for income tax on your net profit

sections.title: ABN Registration & Privacy
paragraphs: Yes — you need an ABN to operate compliantly. The concern we hear most from creators is privacy: 'Will my ABN reveal my home address?'

The answer is no — with proper planning. We regularly help creators register their ABN using a business address or virtual office, keeping personal residential details off public records. This is a standard and ATO-compliant approach.

sections.title: The GST Complexity for OnlyFans
paragraphs: OnlyFans is an international platform, which makes GST classification genuinely complex. The key rules are:
bullets: If your annual turnover exceeds $75,000, you must register for GST
The treatment of platform fees, commissions and cross-border payments must be assessed carefully
Certain transactions may qualify as Exported Services (GST-free); others may not

sections.title: Common OnlyFans GST Errors
paragraphs: Incorrectly treating GST-exempt income as taxable — or the reverse — can result in thousands of dollars of over- or under-payment.
Many creators lodge BAS without understanding which portions of their OnlyFans income attract GST. This is one of the most common and costly errors we see.

sections.title: Tax Deductions for OnlyFans Creators
paragraphs: The following are generally deductible — provided the expense is directly related to your content business and properly documented:
The ATO requires substantiation. A well-maintained expense log with receipts is essential — and we can set this up for you in Xero or similar software from day one.
bullets: Camera, lighting, ring lights, tripods and filming equipment
Computer, tablet or phone used for content creation (business portion)
Editing software subscriptions
Internet and phone bill (business use portion)
Platform fees charged by OnlyFans
Costumes, props and professional makeup used exclusively for filming
Home office expenses if you create content from home
Professional services — accountant, bookkeeper fees
Personal clothing worn outside of content creation
General grooming or beauty treatments not tied to specific content
Gym memberships (unless the fitness content is the actual product)
Living expenses — rent, groceries, general utilities

sections.title: Income Tax — What Rate Will You Pay?
paragraphs: 2024–25 Individual Income Tax Rates
$0 – $18,200:       0%  (tax-free threshold)
Over $180,000:       45% on each dollar above $180,000
Note: Medicare Levy (2%) applies to most taxpayers
As your income grows, the marginal tax rate increases. This is why tax planning — including whether to operate through a company — becomes increasingly valuable at higher income levels.

sections.title: Should You Set Up a Company?
paragraphs: A company structure (taxed at a flat 25% rate for base rate entities) can be beneficial when your net income consistently exceeds approximately $80,000–$120,000. Potential advantages include:

However, PSI (Personal Services Income) rules may limit income splitting if the ATO determines your income is derived primarily from your personal activities — which is very often the case for content creators. Professional structuring advice is essential before setting up a company.
bullets: Capped 25% company tax rate vs up to 47% personal rate
Ability to retain profits within the company for reinvestment
Greater separation between business and personal assets

sections.title: Why Acting Early Matters
paragraphs: OnlyFans income often involves high cash flow, minimal physical inventory, and relatively clean transactions — making it actually well-suited for professional bookkeeping from the start. By engaging a CA early, you can:
bullets: Set up automated bookkeeping so you can focus on creating
Ensure your ABN and privacy setup is correct from registration
Plan for quarterly BAS and income tax instalments before they become a surprise
Avoid the most common — and costly — compliance errors

takeawayItems: Is OnlyFans Income a Business or a Hobby?

card.title: OnlyFans 创作者澳洲报税全指南
card.excerpt: 保护隐私 · 合规申报 · 最大化税务优势

zh.intro: OnlyFans 的收入增长往往很快，随之而来的税务责任也同样快速积累。ATO 已大幅加强对数字平台和订阅制内容收入的监管，合规已经不是可选项，而是必选项。 好消息是：通过正确的税务结构，你完全可以保护个人隐私、合法降低税负，同时保持全面合规。下面是你需要了解的核心内容。

sections.title: OnlyFans 收入算"商业"还是"爱好"？
paragraphs: 根据 ATO 的判断标准，OnlyFans 收入几乎在所有情况下都被认定为商业收入，而非个人爱好——即便你的订阅量还在增长、收入不稳定，或者只是兼职。一旦你为内容换取报酬，这就是商业行为。
实际影响：
bullets: 所有收入必须在年度税表中申报
应以 Sole Trader 身份注册 ABN
需就净利润缴纳所得税

sections.title: ABN 注册与隐私保护
paragraphs: 是的——合规经营需要 ABN。我们最常听到创作者的顾虑是："ABN 会不会暴露我的家庭住址？"
答案是不会——只要提前规划。我们会定期帮助创作者使用商业地址或虚拟办公室注册 ABN，确保个人住宅信息不出现在公开记录中。这是一种标准且符合 ATO 规范的做法。

sections.title: OnlyFans 的 GST 处理有多复杂？
paragraphs: OnlyFans 是一个国际平台，这使得 GST 分类变得相当复杂。核心规则如下：
bullets: 年营业额超过 $75,000，必须注册 GST
平台抽佣、手续费及跨境收款的税务处理需要逐项评估
部分交易可能属于"出口服务"（免 GST），部分则不适用

sections.title: OnlyFans GST 常见错误
paragraphs: 将免 GST 收入误作应税收入申报（或反之）可能导致数千澳元的多缴或少缴。
许多创作者在不清楚哪部分 OnlyFans 收入需要缴纳 GST 的情况下申报 BAS，这是我们最常见到的高频错误之一。

sections.title: OnlyFans 创作者可以申报的扣税项目
paragraphs: 以下费用在符合直接业务用途且有完整记录的前提下，通常可以申报扣除：
ATO 要求提供凭证。维护一份附有收据的费用记录至关重要——我们可以从一开始就帮你在 Xero 等软件中建立这套系统。
bullets: 相机、补光灯、环形灯、三脚架及拍摄设备
用于内容创作的电脑、平板或手机（业务比例部分）
剪辑软件订阅费
网络和手机账单（业务使用比例）
OnlyFans 收取的平台手续费
专门用于拍摄的服装、道具和专业化妆品
居家办公费用（如在家创作内容）
专业服务费——会计师、记账员等
在内容创作以外场合穿着的日常服装
与具体内容无关的日常美容或护理消费
健身会员（除非健身内容本身就是产品）
生活费用——房租、购物、日常水电费

sections.title: 所得税——你将缴纳多少？
paragraphs: 2024–25 个人所得税税率表
超过 $180,000：     超出部分按 45% 缴税
注：大多数纳税人还需缴纳 2% 医疗保险税（Medicare Levy）
随着收入增加，边际税率也在提升。这正是税务规划——包括是否通过公司运营——在高收入阶段越来越有价值的原因。

sections.title: 应该注册公司吗？
paragraphs: 当净收入稳定超过约 $80,000–$120,000 时，公司结构（基本税率实体的固定税率为 25%）可能更为有利。主要优势包括：
但需注意：如果 ATO 认定你的收入属于 PSI（Personal Services Income）——即主要来源于个人活动，内容创作者通常属于这一类——通过公司分配收入的策略可能受到限制。设立公司前，专业的结构规划建议必不可少。
bullets: 固定 25% 公司税率，对比个人最高 47% 税率
利润可以留存于公司内用于再投资
更清晰地分隔业务资产与个人资产

sections.title: 为什么要尽早行动？
paragraphs: OnlyFans 收入通常具有现金流高、无实体库存、账目相对清晰的特点——这恰恰非常适合从一开始就建立专业记账体系。尽早委托注册会计师，你可以：
bullets: 建立自动化记账流程，让你专注于内容创作
从注册之初就确保 ABN 和隐私配置正确
提前规划季度 BAS 和所得税分期付款，避免意外惊喜
规避最常见、也是代价最高的合规错误

takeawayItems: OnlyFans 收入算"商业"还是"爱好"？
OnlyFans 的 GST 处理有多复杂？
